title: Pyrus meuselii
intro: Pyrus meuselii, also known as the Münster pear, is a species of pear tree native to Europe. The health benefits of Pyrus meuselii are attributed to its antiviral, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful in the treatment of various health conditions, including respiratory issues and gastrointestinal problems. The bioactive constituents of Pyrus meuselii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pyrus meuselii are teas and infusions, as well as extracts and tinctures, which are used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of illness.
taxonomy: Pyrus meuselii is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is classified as a member of the genus Pyrus, which includes various species of pear trees. The genus Pyrus is further classified under the subfamily Amygdaloideae, which contains several species of stone fruits and pome fruits. Pyrus meuselii is native to Europe and is a relatively rare species. The classification of Pyrus meuselii is subject to ongoing research and revision in the field of botany.
